subject: Ecology
title: What Are Coral Reefs?
description: A micro-lesson that makes What Are Coral Reefs? usable.

hook.text: Coral reefs look like underwater gardens, but they're actually built by tiny animals.

buildup.text: Coral polyps are soft-bodied creatures that secrete limestone skeletons over centuries.

discovery.text: Inside each polyp live algae called zooxanthellae. They trade sugar for shelter—a mutualism.

twist.text: That partnership gives corals their color. Without the algae, corals turn white and starve.

climax.text: Reefs cover less than 1% of the ocean floor but support roughly 25% of all marine species.

punchline.text: Tiny animals build cities for millions.

quiz.question: What are coral reefs primarily built by?
